summary: Phase 3 (LMS / Learning Materials) backend tests: 28 NEW tests PASS / 0 FAIL. Full regression: Phase 1 (21 pass, 1 skip) + Phase 2 (21 pass, 1 skip) + Phase 3 (28 pass) = 70 PASS / 2 SKIP (same 2 cross-owner-delete skips carried from iteration_4/_5 — unchanged seed-data limitation). Every requested check verified: admin list grouped-by-subject with correct icon/color, YouTube watch+shorts+youtu.be+watch?v=&list= all resolve to https://www.youtube.com/embed/UPBMG5EYydo, Vimeo resolves to player.vimeo.com/video/76979871, non-YT/Vimeo video falls back to 'Open the video on the original platform', form validation errors on video without url and pdf without file/url, teacher list shows all materials, teacher field locked (disabled=True) on create form for teacher role and auto-assigned on save, class-scoping for students (Tiago class_id=3 sees TEST_Class3Only, Miguel class_id≠3 does not — and gets 403 on detail), is_published=False drafts invisible to students+parents in list AND return 403 on detail, teacher cannot delete admin-uploaded material (403) while admin can delete anything, and all four cross-portal nav links present with correct data-testid attributes.

minor.issue: Permission check uses `m.teacher.teacher_user_id == u.id`. If a material was created via admin path with `teacher` left blank (m.teacher = None), a teacher who is logged in will be blocked (correct), but the same path also blocks the teacher from deleting a material an admin uploaded with `teacher` pointing to that teacher's own row. Currently nothing distinguishes 'I uploaded this' from 'I was tagged as subject teacher'. Consider adding an `uploaded_by = ForeignKey(User)` audit field. Not blocking — see code review.

When material_type is 'other', no required-field validation runs — an empty 'other' record (no url, no file) can be created and the detail page renders 'No content attached.' as a dead-end. Consider requiring at least one of url/file for 'other' too.
`initial['subject'] = t.subjects.first()` is non-deterministic for teachers who teach more than one subject (ordering depends on DB insert order). Cosmetic.

action_items: (optional) Add an `uploaded_by = ForeignKey(User)` field on LearningMaterial so the 'teacher cannot delete other teacher's material' boundary is identity-based rather than role-tag-based; current logic confuses 'subject teacher' with 'uploader'.
(optional) LearningMaterialForm.clean() should require url-or-file for material_type='other' (currently allows totally empty material).
(optional) Same seed-data nit as iteration_4/_5: add a 2nd LessonPlan and a 2nd Assignment owned by a teacher OTHER than t-004 so the two carried SKIP tests can stop skipping.
critical_code_review_comments: LearningMaterial.embed_url correctly handles all four required YouTube variants (youtu.be/<id>, watch?v=<id>, watch?v=<id>&list=..., shorts/<id>) and Vimeo numeric ids. Verified via 4 parametrized HTTP integration tests — every variant yields the exact substring https://www.youtube.com/embed/UPBMG5EYydo (or player.vimeo.com/video/76979871). Non-YT/Vimeo URLs fall through and return ''.

academy_views.learning_material_list correctly per-role scopes: admin/principal/teacher see everything, student filters with Q(class_room__isnull=True) | Q(class_room=st.school_class) AND is_published=True, parent same pattern over Q(class_room_id__in=children_classes). The detail endpoint additionally re-checks is_published and class membership and returns HttpResponseForbidden — verified end-to-end with Miguel/Tiago/parent sessions.

academy_views.learning_material_create disables the `teacher` field for role=teacher AND overrides m.teacher on save — so a teacher cannot impersonate another teacher by hand-editing the disabled select before submit. Verified: 'TEST_LMS Teacher Owned' created by t-004 shows Carlos Mendes on detail page.

Template _learning_material_detail_body.html branches correctly: m.embed_url → iframe; video without embed_url → 'Open the video on the original platform' fallback; m.file ending in .pdf → inline iframe with #toolbar=0; m.file otherwise → 'Open file' button; m.url-only (link) → 'Open link' button; else → 'No content attached.' Good defensive ladder.

Nav wiring is consistent: admin sidebar via {% include '_nav_item.html' with url='/api/learning/' label='Learning Materials' tid='nav-learning' %}; student/teacher/parent topbars each have a 'Materials' link with role-prefixed data-testid (student-nav-learning, teacher-nav-learning, parent-nav-learning) and the active-route highlight matches when '/learning' is in request.path.

LearningMaterial.icon / .color properties expose the Phosphorus icon class and inline hex correctly per type. The list template uses style="background-color: {{ m.color }}20; color: {{ m.color }}" — clever 12% alpha tint without extra CSS. Note: relies on browsers parsing 8-digit hex (e.g., '#dc262620') which is supported in all modern browsers.

Form validation: clean() correctly enforces url for video/link and url-OR-file for pdf/slides. BUT material_type='other' has no such guard — a totally empty 'other' material is accepted; the detail page then dead-ends with 'No content attached.' Not a security bug, but a usability/data-integrity nit.

Permission boundary on learning_material_delete uses `m.teacher and m.teacher.teacher_user_id == u.id` — this conflates 'teacher tagged on the material' with 'uploader'. An admin can create a material on behalf of teacher X with teacher=X, and then teacher X will be allowed to delete it (which is probably fine). But two teachers who co-teach a subject can't delete each other's stuff only because the foreign-key is single — once we move to multi-teacher subjects this will become a real bug. Adding an uploaded_by audit FK would future-proof this.

success_rate.backend: 100% (28/28 Phase 3, 42/42 Phase 1+2 regression executed; 2 unrelated cross-owner-delete tests skipped by design — pre-existing seed-data limitation)

test_credentials: admin/admin123, t-004/teacher123, adm-1003/student123 (Miguel — Year 7-B), adm-1005/student123 (Tiago — class id=3), parent/password123. All match /app/memory/test_credentials.md.
seed_data_creation: Phase 3 created these TEST_-prefixed LearningMaterial rows (all additive, safe to leave in DB): TEST_LMS YouTube Watch (video, subject=1, no class scope), TEST_LMS YT youtu.be / TEST_LMS YT shorts / TEST_LMS YT watch+list (3 video variants), TEST_LMS Vimeo, TEST_LMS Other Video (fallback), TEST_LMS Teacher Owned (link, owned by t-004), TEST_Class3Only (link, class_room=3 — verifies cross-class isolation), TEST_Draft (link, is_published=False — verifies draft hiding), TEST_LMS Admin Owned For Delete (link, kept), TEST_LMS Admin Delete Target (link, DELETED at end of admin-delete test). One bookkeeping note: TEST_LMS NoURL Video and TEST_LMS NoFile PDF were intentionally form-error attempts and are NOT persisted.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Phase 3 routes: GET /api/learning/ (list, grouped by subject), GET /api/learning/<pk>/ (detail with iframe for YT/Vimeo/PDF), POST /api/learning/add/ (admin+teacher only, teacher field disabled for teachers), POST /api/learning/<pk>/delete/. LearningMaterial.embed_url handles YT (youtu.be, watch?v=, shorts/, watch?v=&list=) and Vimeo numeric ids — extended URL parsing is regex-free but works on all 4 required variants. Audience scoping for students uses Q(class_room__isnull=True) | Q(class_room=st.school_class) AND is_published=True; identical pattern for parents over their children's classes. Cross-portal nav: admin sidebar 'Learning Materials' + student/teacher/parent topbar 'Materials' (each with data-testid={role}-nav-learning). Seed data left in DB is TEST_-prefixed, additive, and safe. Phase 1+2 regressions still pass 100% of executed tests. SendGrid remains MOCKED via erp/mail.py.
